# UC-17.3.64 · Netskope DLP Policy Violations and Sensitive Data Exfiltration Attempts

## Description

Netskope DLP identifies regulated data movement toward unsanctioned SaaS instances, personal webmail, or generative AI tools. Tracking violations by rule profile and enforcement action proves control effectiveness for privacy and sector regulators.

## Value

Produces investigator-ready evidence for insider risk and accidental exfiltration without decrypting full payloads in Splunk.

## Implementation

Ensure DLP dictionaries and exact data match fields are enabled in tenant policy. Mask or hash sensitive literals at ingestion if required by legal. Route critical severities to a restricted index with stricter RBAC. Quarterly review false positives with data owners.

## SPL

```spl
index=casb sourcetype="netskope:events" earliest=-7d
| eval dlp=coalesce(dlp_profile, dlp_rule, policy_name)
| eval sev=lower(coalesce(severity, dlp_severity, "medium"))
| where match(lower(coalesce(activity, ni_activity, "")),"(?i)upload|post|share|create|edit") AND match(lower(dlp),"(?i)dlp|confidential|pci|pii|secret")
| stats count by user, app_name, dlp, action, sev
| sort -count
| head 40
```

## Visualization

Table (user, application, DLP profile), Heatmap (severity × action), Sankey (app → destination domain).

- [Splunkbase — Netskope Add-on for Splunk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3808)
